behavior_summary: U8P_ORLOK implements an NPC 'Orlok' who functions as a barkeep: it provides a short 'U8P_ORLOK::look' description, idle/proximity barks, responds to assaults, and runs a conversation/shop flow when the player 'uses' (talks to) him.
U8P_ORLOK::look (process) shows corpse text if Orlok is dead; otherwise it selects a short label based on flags (orlokName, orlokJob) and falls back to a generic 'man'.

Periodic and proximity behavior is driven by U8P_ORLOK::func02DC and U8P_ORLOK::func049C: a random selector plus distance checks produce idle lines, short animations, and occasional spawned helper tasks.
Area lifecycle handlers U8P_ORLOK::enterFastArea and U8P_ORLOK::leaveFastArea clear transient references; U8P_ORLOK::leaveFastArea additionally teleports a dead corpse to randomized coordinates.
U8P_ORLOK::cast reacts to being targeted/assaulted: it retries acquiring a target by spawning a helper, calls the guard system on confirmed assault, resets some references, and emits an alarm bark.

Conversation/shop entry U8P_ORLOK::use builds a dynamic menu of dialog and shop options (using flags like orlokMet, orlokTales, orlokDrink), presents it to the player, and delegates purchase choice handling to U8P_ORLOK::func0CD7.

Shop utilities U8P_ORLOK::func0BCF and U8P_ORLOK::func0C74 format drink/item name strings and compute a price string respectively; U8P_ORLOK::func0CD7 constructs the drink/shop menu, captures the player's pick, and sets selection/cost state.
notable_control_flow: U8P_ORLOK::look first checks Npc::isDead(this) — corpse path always takes precedence and short-circuits other label logic.
U8P_ORLOK::func02DC chooses behavior via a random selector (urandom) and branches: some branches run short animation/timing sequences, others call U8P_ORLOK::func049C when nobody is talking.
U8P_ORLOK::func049C gates its barks on proximity (FREE::getDistanceBetween < threshold) and on orlokMet to vary first-meeting wording.

U8P_ORLOK::cast loops up to a retry limit (local1) spawning FREE::func1C19 until Npc::getTarget yields a value; a target==1 triggers CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime and an assault bark if Orlok is alive.

U8P_ORLOK::use builds and mutates a menu array (MenuSet, MenuUnion, MenuRemove, MenuAdd), presents it via Ask, then routes the suspend-assigned choice (strcmp) into separate dialog/shop handling — purchases are processed in U8P_ORLOK::func0CD7.
open_questions: Are whatDrink and cost stored globally or scoped to the conversation? (script treats whatDrink like a persistent selector; exact storage scope is inferred).
What exact game state(s) set orlokDrink/whatDrink/haveAnother across game sessions — are they persistent across map unloads? (persistence unknown).
The exact semantics and side effects of FREE::func1C19 (spawned helper) are unclear beyond being a short delayed/background task.
Whether some numeric local fields (local3 in U8P_ORLOK::func0CD7) map directly to item IDs vs. text ids is inferred but not confirmed.
Are any other scripts or systems expected to set flags like orlokMet or avatarTooCheap outside this script? (integration unknown).

main_function.name: U8P_ORLOK::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines Orlok; show corpse text if dead, otherwise pick a name/occupation label based on orlokName and orlokJob flags (falling back to 'man').
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player's 'U8P_ORLOK::look' or examine action on the NPC.
Runs whenever the engine invokes the NPC's U8P_ORLOK::look handler (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_ORLOK::look
function_map.role: short-description handler for inspecting Orlok; corpse handling and name/job label selection.

function_map.name: U8P_ORLOK::enterFastArea
function_map.role: clear transient references and kick off periodic idle behavior (calls U8P_ORLOK::func02DC) when an actor enters Orlok's fast-update area.

function_map.name: U8P_ORLOK::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: clear transient references and, if dead, teleport the corpse to randomized coordinates (cleanup on area leave).

function_map.name: U8P_ORLOK::cast
function_map.role: handle being targeted or assaulted: retry for target, spawn helpers, call guard response and bark when attacked.

function_map.name: U8P_ORLOK::func02DC
function_map.role: periodic/random idle-behavior driver deciding between animations, small waits, spawns, or invoking the idle dialog driver.

function_map.name: U8P_ORLOK::func049C
function_map.role: idle/proximity monologue driver: proximity-gated random barks and small spawn/timing sequences; uses orlokMet to vary wording for first meeting.

function_map.name: U8P_ORLOK::func0BCF
function_map.role: format/lookup drink name strings based on a selector (whatDrink); returns a short name fragment for dialog insertion.

function_map.name: U8P_ORLOK::func0C74
function_map.role: compute/format a numeric cost based on a drink selector (whatDrink) and return it as text (numToStr used).

function_map.name: U8P_ORLOK::func0CD7
function_map.role: shop/menu builder and handler: populate options, Ask the player, capture the choice, set cost/selection flags and prepare purchase state.

function_map.name: U8P_ORLOK::use
function_map.role: primary conversation/dialog entrypoint: build dialog/shop menu, greet the player (orlokMet handling), present options, and delegate purchases to U8P_ORLOK::func0CD7.

internal_calls.purpose: Start the periodic/random idle behavior after clearing references.
When Orlok is dead, teleport to randomized coordinates to remove/relocate corpse (teleport is external but triggered here).
Spawn retry helper processes while waiting for Npc::getTarget to return a target (spawned helper used as a short delay/backoff).
Invoke idle monologue driver when conditions (nobodyTalking, random branch) select that path.
Delegate drink/shop menu construction and purchase handling to the shop handler.
Inline compute/format a cost string for presentation in dialog (e.g., 'That'll be {U8P_ORLOK::func0C74} black chips').
Produce a short drink name fragment for follow-up dialog when the player asks for another drink.

external_calls.purpose: Determine whether the corpse-specific U8P_ORLOK::look text should be shown.
Move a dead NPC to random coordinates (corpse cleanup).
Obtain the NPC's current target to detect an assault (compared against literal value 1).
Notify guard system when an assault is confirmed (target == 1).
Trigger a short animation/gesture or pre-speech action before barks or timing steps.
Insert small timing/wait steps between animations, spawns, and barks.
Check player proximity to gate proximity barks.
Pick a random branch for probabilistic idle behavior.
Convert numeric cost to a string for display.
Clear transient reference slots when a player enters the NPC's fast-update area.
Clear transient reference slots on area exit.
Clear conversation/target references when assaulted and still alive.
Spawn a delayed/helper task in some idle branches.
Obtain the avatar's name for personalized dialog strings.

reads.name: orlokName
reads.likely_effect: Select whether to include Orlok's personal name label in U8P_ORLOK::look and to decide offering 'what is your name' in menus.

reads.name: orlokJob
reads.likely_effect: Select whether to include occupation label ('barkeep') in U8P_ORLOK::look and to add job-related menu options.

reads.name: orlokMet
reads.likely_effect: Distinguish first-meeting vs repeat greetings in idle and conversation branches (changes wording).

reads.name: orlokTales
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether tale/story menu entries are offered in conversation.

reads.name: orlokDrink
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether drink-related options are presented and affects greeting lines.

reads.name: orlokTab
reads.likely_effect: Used to decide whether to offer 'What is my tab?' and to report current tab totals in dialog.

reads.name: orlokAvaName
reads.likely_effect: Determines whether Orlok uses the avatar's real name (getName) or generic 'stranger' in dialog.

reads.name: orlokPoison
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether a 'What do you mean by poison?' menu option is added.

reads.name: haveAnother
reads.likely_effect: Used as a short-circuit to reuse a prior drink selection path in the shop flow (checked by U8P_ORLOK::func0CD7).

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: When true, prevents triggering of idle monologue U8P_ORLOK::func049C from U8P_ORLOK::func02DC (used to avoid interrupting active conversations).

reads.name: avatarTooCheap
reads.likely_effect: Read in the Goodbye branch to produce special behavior or wording if previously flagged; it is cleared on goodbye.

writes.name: orlokMet
writes.likely_effect: Set to true when the player first meets Orlok (affects future greeting wording).

writes.name: whatDrink
writes.likely_effect: Written when a drink selection is made to record which drink was chosen (used in quick-reuse paths).

writes.name: haveAnother
writes.likely_effect: Cleared or set to implement the 'have another' shortcut in the shop flow (U8P_ORLOK::func0CD7 references).

writes.name: avatarTooCheap
writes.likely_effect: Cleared (set to 0) on Goodbye to reset that special-case flag.

writes.name: orlokDrink
writes.likely_effect: Set when the dialog branch offers or records a drink-related interaction (marks state for subsequent dialog).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_ORLOK implements an NPC 'Orlok' who functions as a barkeep: it provides a short 'U8P_ORLOK::look' description, idle/proximity barks, responds to assaults, and runs a conversation/shop flow when the player 'uses' (talks to) him.
